title: eryngium trifolium
intro: Eryngium trifolium, also known as Bells of Ireland, is a flowering plant native to Ireland and other parts of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce pain and swelling. The plant's health benefits include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, Eryngium trifolium can be found in teas, tinctures, and salves, often combined with other herbs for its medicinal properties.
